skillName: Generate linear sequences using term to term rules
misconceptionSummary: Students can produce terms but do not see term-to-term rules as local rules depending on the previous term.

visuals.ruleText: A term-to-term rule tells you how to get from one term to the next
visuals.subText: You need the previous term each time

steps.narration: A term-to-term rule is local. It depends on the term before.
Each term depends on the previous one like links in a chain.

lines.text: If the rule is +4, the difference stays 4

steps.narration: That is why a constant term-to-term add rule creates a linear sequence.

lines.text: Nth term uses the position number directly

steps.narration: This is different from an nth-term rule.

visuals.expression: Term-to-term rules build sequences step by step
visuals.label: Each term comes from the one before

steps.narration: The big idea is that term-to-term generation is sequential, not direct from position.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: mixing up term-to-term rules and nth-term rules. Term-to-term rules depend on the previous term.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If you need the last term to find the next one, you are using a term-to-term rule.
